claim: Prediction in atom ATOM_T010_S008_PRE_01 ("Okay, so what's gonna happen in this little model is if it wants to produce a...") holds if and only if the first-principle constraint in atom ATOM_T007_S015_FIR_01 ("So this is what's fundamentally driving the Gold Rush that we see today in Co...") is binding.
first_principles_validity_hypothesis: The prediction "Okay, so what's gonna happen in this little model is if i..." is forced by the underlying constraint "So this is what's fundamentally driving the Gold Rush tha..."; if the constraint is not binding the prediction may still hold by accident but the binding case is the load-bearing version of this candidate.
why_novel_vs_speaker: The speaker introduces the prediction (snippet S008) and the first-principle (snippet S015) in separate contexts; the speaker does not state that the second forces the first.
why_potentially_useful: A prediction that is forced by a first-principle is more falsifiable than a free-floating prediction: if the principle holds and the prediction fails, one of them is wrong, which is a binary research question.
